title: Guest Support Escalation Matrix for Hotels in Egypt
trigger: Guest issue unresolved after initial support contact

steps.title: Initial Guest Contact and Issue Logging
steps.action: Front desk staff receives guest complaint and logs the issue in the PMS. Ensure all relevant details are captured including guest name, room number, and nature of the complaint.

steps.title: Attempt Resolution at First Contact
steps.action: Front desk staff attempts to resolve the issue immediately if possible. This includes providing information, offering alternatives, or compensating with amenities.

steps.title: Escalate to Supervisor
steps.action: If the issue is not resolved at the first contact, escalate to the shift supervisor. The supervisor reviews the case details and contacts the guest to offer further assistance.

steps.title: Escalate to Hotel Manager
steps.action: If the supervisor is unable to resolve the issue, escalate to the hotel manager. The manager will assess the situation and determine an appropriate course of action, which may include offering compensation or further investigation.

steps.title: Resolution Confirmation and Guest Follow-Up
steps.action: Confirm resolution with the guest and ensure satisfaction. Follow up with a courtesy call or email to thank the guest for their patience and to confirm that the issue has been resolved to their satisfaction.

steps.title: Escalate to Corporate Support
steps.action: If the hotel manager cannot resolve the issue, escalate to corporate support for further assistance. Provide all documentation and communication records to corporate support for review.

description: This playbook outlines the escalation matrix for handling guest complaints in hotels operating in Egypt. It ensures timely resolution of issues and enhances guest satisfaction by providing a structured approach to problem-solving.
